rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN MALLOY VS. CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice In Compliance With Cc Section 1005
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 21, 2025, Line 1, 1- PLAINTIFF STEPHEN MALLOY's Notice In Compliance With CC Section 1005. Off calendar. The "Notice" does not appear to be a motion of any kind. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN MALLOY VS. CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Hearing Notice Tuesday Janury 21 At 9:30am With Dept. 301 Copies. In Compliance With Cc Setion 1005 And Local Rule
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 21, 2025, Line 1, 2- PETITIONER STEPHEN MALLOY's Re-Hearing Notice Tuesday Janury 21 At 9:30am With Dept. 301 Copies. In Compliance With CC Setion 1005 And Local Rule. Denied. Plaintiff has not properly filed moving papers requesting any form of relief this court can grant. It is unclear from this "re-hearing notice" what relief, if any, Plaintiff presently seeks. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. rulings.Case Title: LATAYA ROBINSON VS. CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 21, 2025, Line 4, DEFENDANT AMONE CORP.'s Motion for Summary Judgment Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication. (Part 1 of 2 for purposes of entry of tentative ruling.) Defendant AmOne's motion for summary judgment and motion for summary adjudication on the first cause of action for premises liability/negligence are denied and its motion for summary adjudication on the second cause of action for willful failure to warn is granted. Defendant failed to maintain its initial summary motion burden as to the first cause of action. Defendant argues that it contractually assumed only one duty as to the elevator-"to remove the current elevator and install a new elevator in its place." (UMF 14.) To support this assertion, the defendant proffers the Standard Form of Agreement Between Owner and Contractor Document. (Defendant's Ex. 5.) Article I of that agreement provides that: "The Contract Documents consist of this modified AIA Document A102-2017 Agreement ("Agreement"), the Exhibits to this Agreement, Conditions of the Contract (General, Supplementary, and other Conditions, as modified." Defendant only attached as exhibit 5 the Standard Form of Agreement Between Owner and Contractor Document. None of the other cited documents (including the referenced General Conditions) are attached and there is no clear evidence that the Standard Form of Agreement Between Owner and Contractor Document constitutes the entire agreement. (See Wong Decl., par. 6b.) It is therefore unclear exactly what contractual duties the defendant owed and thus it failed to maintain its burden. The court notes that the plaintiff proffers a copy of General Conditions and Article 10 of those conditions show a duty to provide safety conditions. (Plaintiff's Ex. 3.) The court sustains the defendant's objection to exhibit 3 based on lack of foundation and authentication since plaintiff's counsel Padway proffers the General Conditions and he lacks personal knowledge regarding the terms of defendant's agreement. (Padway Decl., par. 4.) In any event, the defendant failed to maintain its burden. As an aside, evidence indicates that the defendant was aware that the ceiling tiles were sitting unattached prior to the accident. (Plaintiff's Ex. 1 [Wong Depo., 57:13-58:23].) The second cause of action for willful failure to warn is assertedly based on Civil Code section 846. That code section provides that the immunity to property owners when they open up their property for recreational purposesdoes not apply where there is a willful failure to warn. This theory of liability is inapposite in this case. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. (Tentative Ruling continues in Part 2 of 2) =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PRINCESS ABROGAR VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendants Requests For Documents, Set Two, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Her Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 21, 2025 Line 2. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendants Requests For Documents, Set Two, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Her Counsel. Granted. Plaintiff must serve code-compliant verified further responses to document requests 27-33 without objections and produce all responsive documents no later than January 27, 2025. Plaintiff and her counsel must pay $3,000 in reasonable monetary sanctions to the City and County of San Francisco no later than February 20, 2025. The City has shown good cause for the relief it seeks and the plaintiff's arguments against that relief lack substantial justification. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS F CARLSON ET AL VS. PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion, Motion, And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Strike Or, In The Alternative, To Seal Portions Of Defendant Pg&Es Motion Papers
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 21, 2025, Line 9, PLAINTIFFS DOUGLAS CARLSON and MAYA ZUBKOVSKAYA's Motion To Strike Or, In The Alternative, To Seal Portions Of Defendant PG&Es Motion Papers. Plaintiffs' motion to strike and alternative motion to seal are denied. Using the names of the two drugs is necessary for a fair and efficient resolution of the parties' dispute and plaintiffs have not shown how they will suffer legal prejudice from the use of those names. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: JOHN CRESS VS. SPEAR INVESTMENT GROUP, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion, Motion And Memorandum In Support Of Cross-Defendant John Cress' Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Cost Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16 (Anti-Slapp Statute)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 21, 2025, Line 10, CROSS-DEFENDANT JOHN CRESS' Motion For Attorney's Fees And Cost Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16 (Anti-Slapp Statute). Plaintiff John Cress' motion for fees and costs is granted in small part. Mr. Cress is awarded $10,000 in fees, calculated as 25 hours of attorney time at $400 per hour, and $373 in costs for a total of $10,373. Review of the anti-SLAPP motion shows that it was a simple one that could and should have been handled within 20 hours by an experienced attorney charging no more than $400 per hour. An experienced attorney at the same hourly rate should require no more than five hours to prepare this fees motion. This motion appears to be part of a disturbing trend of fees motions after successful anti-SLAPP motions against pro per pleaders that seek exorbitant amounts of money that no reasonable client would ever pay for filing anti-SLAPP motions based on settled rules. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: BIANCA VISCUSI VS. FIREFLY HEALTH CORP, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 21, 2025, Line 11, DEFENDANTS FIREFLY HEALTH CORP, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION DOING BUSINESS AS VELVET CANNABIS, AND FARID HARRISON'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants Firefly Health Corp. and Farid Harrison's demurrer to Plaintiff Bianca Viscusi's first amended complaint (FAC) is sustained without leave to amend. Defendants Firefly and Harrison demur to Plaintiff's intentional infliction of emotional distress (IIED) claim in the FAC. This court sustained, with leave to amend, Defendants' demurrer to the original IIED claim on grounds Plaintiff failed to allege extreme and outrageous conduct, and failed to allege severe emotional distress. (See 8/8/24 Order in ROA.) Defendants demur to this IIED claim, once again contending Plaintiff fails to state a claim. (CCP 430.10(e).) The FAC suffers from the same deficiencies as the original complaint, namely failure to allege extreme and outrageous conduct. Defendants represent that the only new paragraphs in the FAC are paragraphs 2, 3, 11-37, and Plaintiffs do not disagree. None of these revised or added paragraphs contain new material allegations of Defendants' purported extreme and outrageous conduct. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom.
